Bash(python3 -c "
from math import log, exp

# OK so the ADJUSTMENT_HEAD basically only sees the = sign for answer positions
# because the ALiBi decay makes bos and + negligible.
# But wait - what about digit tokens? They have K=-1000, which is way more negative.
# And the other 4 heads \(0,1,2\) have ALiBi slope = 0, so no distance penalty.

# Let me look at ALL heads, not just ADJUSTMENT_HEAD
# Heads 0,1,2 have slopes = 0
# Head 3 \(ADJUSTMENT\) has slope = log\(10\)
# Head 4 \(SCALE\) has slope = 0

# For heads with slope 0:
# score = q*k = 1 * k_value
# digits: 1 * \(-1000\) = -1000
# specials: 1 * \(-40\) = -40
# So even for heads 0,1,2,4: attention goes to special tokens only

# But with softmax1, the denominator is 1 + sum\(exp\), so if all scores are very negative,
# the output is near 0.

# Let's check: with 3 special tokens each scoring -40:
# softmax1 weight for each = exp\(-40\) / \(1 + 3*exp\(-40\)\) ≈ exp\(-40\) ≈ 4.25e-18
# So effectively zero! softmax1 returns ~0 for all tokens.

# For ADJUSTMENT_HEAD at answer positions:
# = token is at distance 0,1,2,... from answer positions
# For the = token at pos 22, from query at pos 22: score = -40 + 0 = -40
# From query at pos 23: score = -40 + log\(10\)*\(-1\) = -40 - 2.3 = -42.3
# All still very small with softmax1

# So heads 0,1,2 contribute ~0 to residual
# Head 3 \(ADJUSTMENT\) and Head 4 \(SCALE\) contribute small values

# Wait - but the V values are scaled by V_PROJ_SCALE = exp\(K_SPECIAL_SCORE - log\(10\)\)
# V_PROJ_SCALE = exp\(-40 - 2.3\) = exp\(-42.3\) ≈ 4.25e-19
# And the V projections divide by this! So v0_w1 = 0.1 / exp\(-42.3\)

V_PROJ_SCALE = exp\(-40 - log\(10\)\)
print\(f'V_PROJ_SCALE = {V_PROJ_SCALE:.4e}'\)
print\(f'v0_w1 = 0.1 / V_PROJ_SCALE = {0.1/V_PROJ_SCALE:.4e}'\)
print\(f'v0_w2 = -1.1 / V_PROJ_SCALE = {-1.1/V_PROJ_SCALE:.4e}'\)

# So the V values for special tokens are HUGE, but the attention weights are TINY \(exp\(-40\)\)
# The product V*attn should cancel out to something reasonable

# For the = token \(SPECIAL=1, EQ=1\):
# V_adj = 0.1/V_PROJ_SCALE + \(-1.1\)/V_PROJ_SCALE = -1.0/V_PROJ_SCALE
# For +, bos tokens \(SPECIAL=1, EQ=0\):
# V_adj = 0.1/V_PROJ_SCALE

# Attention weight for = at pos 22 from query at pos 22+k:
# Using softmax1: exp\(-40 - log\(10\)*k\) / \(1 + sum_of_all_exp_scores\)
# The dominant term in the sum is the = sign itself, so approximately:
# attn\(=\) ≈ exp\(-40 - log\(10\)*k\) / \(1 + exp\(-40-log\(10\)*k\) + ...\) ≈ exp\(-40 - log\(10\)*k\) for small values

# Actually with softmax1, when all scores << 0, attn ≈ exp\(score\) directly

# So attn_adj\(=\) * V_adj\(=\) ≈ exp\(-40 - log\(10\)*k\) * \(-1.0/exp\(-40 - log\(10\)\)\)
# = exp\(-40 - k*log\(10\)\) / exp\(-40 - log\(10\)\) 
# = exp\(-40 - k*log\(10\) + 40 + log\(10\)\)
# = exp\(\(1-k\)*log\(10\)\)
# = 10^\(1-k\)

# THAT'S THE KEY! The adjustment head contributes 10^\(1-k\) scaled by -1.0 for the = sign
# Wait, plus contributions from bos and +...

# For bos at pos 0, from query at pos 22+k:
# attn * V = exp\(-40 - log\(10\)*\(22+k\)\) * \(0.1/exp\(-40-log\(10\)\)\)
# = 0.1 * exp\(-\(22+k\)*log\(10\) + log\(10\)\)
# = 0.1 * 10^\(1-22-k\) = 0.1 * 10^\(-21-k\) -- negligible!

# For + at pos 11:
# attn * V = exp\(-40 - log\(10\)*\(11+k\)\) * \(0.1/exp\(-40-log\(10\)\)\)
# = 0.1 * 10^\(1-11-k\) = 0.1 * 10^\(-10-k\) -- also tiny for answer digits

# So the ADJUSTMENT_HEAD output at answer position 22+k is dominated by:
# ≈ \(-1.0\) * 10^\(1-k\) / exp\(0\) ... 

# Hmm wait, let me be more careful about softmax1
# softmax1\(x\) = exp\(x_i\) / \(1 + sum\(exp\(x_j\)\)\)
# For query at pos 22+k, the sum is over all positions 0..22+k
# Most are digit tokens with score -1000, so exp\(-1000\) ≈ 0
# Special tokens: bos, +, = with scores around -40 to -90
# So denominator ≈ 1 + exp\(-40-k*log10\) + tiny_terms ≈ 1

# So attn_weight\(=\) ≈ exp\(-40 - k*log\(10\)\)
# And V\(=\) = -1.0 / exp\(-40 - log\(10\)\) 
# Product = exp\(-40 - k*log10\) * \(-1.0\) / exp\(-40 - log10\) = -1.0 * exp\(\(1-k\)*log10\) = -10^\(1-k\)

print\(\)
print\('ADJUSTMENT_HEAD contribution at answer positions \(from = sign only\):'\)
for k in range\(5\):
    val = -1.0 * 10**\(1-k\)
    print\(f'  Answer digit {k}: ≈ {val:.4f}'\)

# And for SCALE_HEAD \(head 4\), ALiBi slope = 0, so all positions get same distance penalty
# V is only nonzero for = sign \(EQ_DIM=1, v0_w3=1.0\)
# Score for = is -40, attn ≈ exp\(-40\), V = 1.0
# Product ≈ exp\(-40\) ≈ 4.25e-18 -- negligible!

print\(\)
print\(f'SCALE_HEAD contribution: exp\(-40\) = {exp\(-40\):.4e} -- negligible'\)
")

import pyray as rl
from vis_adder import run_and_capture, build_layout, draw_tensor_block, draw_flow_arrow, value_to_color, BG_COLOR
from tinyadder_module import TinyAdderModule

model = TinyAdderModule\(\)
tensors = run_and_capture\(model, 1234, 5678, 8\)
blocks = build_layout\(tensors\)

# Just test that draw functions don't crash \(can't actually render without display\)
# Test color mapping
for v in [-10, -1, 0, 1, 10, 50, 90]:
    c = value_to_color\(v, -10, 90\)
    print\(f'val={v:5} -> RGBA\({c.r},{c.g},{c.b},{c.a}\)'\)

print\('All smoke tests passed'\)
")

Bash(python -c "
from vis_adder import fmt_large, fmt_sci, fmt_small, fmt_decimal, MAX_CHARS

# L0 FFN = row values \(trillions range\)
tests = [
    \('fmt_large', fmt_large, [-208.3e9, -625.0e9, -1042e9, -3958.3e9, 10000, 50000, 80000, 0, -3958, 1234567]\),
    \('fmt_sci', fmt_sci, [1.17e9, 1.95e9, 26.95e9, 0, 7.5e6, 4e18]\),
    \('fmt_small', fmt_small, [0, 1, 10, 90, 0.042, -10.0]\),
    \('fmt_decimal', fmt_decimal, [0, 0.001, 0.0312, 1.0, -0.042]\),
]
ok = True
for name, fn, vals in tests:
    for v in vals:
        s = fn\(v\)
        flag = ' ***TOO LONG***' if len\(s\) > MAX_CHARS else ''
        if flag: ok = False
        print\(f'  {name}\({v:>15}\) = {s!r:>{MAX_CHARS+2}s} \({len\(s\)} chars\){flag}'\)
    print\(\)
print\('All OK' if ok else 'FAILURES FOUND'\)
")

Bash(python -c "
from vis_adder import run_and_capture, build_layout
from tinyadder_module import TinyAdderModule
model = TinyAdderModule\(\)
tensors = run_and_capture\(model, 1234, 5678, 8\)
blocks = build_layout\(tensors\)
last = blocks[-1]
total_h = last.y + last.dims * 40
total_w = blocks[0].seq_len * 40
print\(f'{len\(blocks\)} blocks'\)
print\(f'Total extent: {total_w:.0f}w x {total_h:.0f}h world units'\)
print\(\)
for b in blocks:
    w = b.seq_len * 40
    h = b.dims * 40
    print\(f'  {b.name}: y={b.y:.0f} [{b.seq_len} cols x {b.dims} rows] = {w}x{h}px'\)
")

Bash(python -c "
# Quick check that draw_tensor_block won't crash with new field names
from vis_adder import run_and_capture, build_layout
from tinyadder_module import TinyAdderModule
model = TinyAdderModule\(\)
tensors = run_and_capture\(model, 1234, 5678, 8\)
blocks = build_layout\(tensors\)
b = blocks[0]
# Check all accessed fields exist
print\(f'name={b.name}, seq_len={b.seq_len}, dims={b.dims}'\)
print\(f'dim_labels={b.dim_labels}'\)
print\(f'argmin_col={b.argmin_col}'\)
print\(f'data[0]={b.data[0]}'\)
print\(f'data[30]={b.data[30]}'\)
# Check argmin block
ab = blocks[-1]
print\(f'argmin block: seq_len={ab.seq_len}, argmin_col={ab.argmin_col}'\)
col_vals = [ab.data[ab.argmin_col][d] for d in range\(ab.dims\)]
min_d = min\(range\(len\(col_vals\)\), key=lambda i: col_vals[i]\)
print\(f'argmin dim={min_d} \(should be 9\)'\)
print\('All OK'\)
")

Bash(python -c "
import tinyadder_4
tinyadder_4.verbose = 0
tinyadder_4.self_test\(n=1000, seed=42\)
# Also test edge cases
model = tinyadder_4.TinyAdder4Module\(\)
edges = [\(0,0\), \(0,1\), \(9999,9999\), \(9999,1\), \(1,9999\), \(5000,5000\), \(9999,0\)]
for a,b in edges:
    r = tinyadder_4.add\(model, a, b\)
    ok = '✓' if r == a+b else '✗'
    print\(f'  {a} + {b} = {r} \(expected {a+b}\) {ok}'\)
")
